title: parse_send_response double-spend test omits message field check
description: In client.rs lines 438-448, the test `parse_send_response_double_spend_returns_broadcast_failed` uses `matches\!(err, BroadcastError::BroadcastFailed { code: 2, .. })` with a `...` wildcard, which does not verify the `message` field is forwarded verbatim. The test doc comment claims it 'tests that the error_message is forwarded verbatim, not truncated', but the assertion does not verify that. Compare with `parse_send_response_error_code_returns_broadcast_failed` (lines 419-431) which uses `assert_eq\!` and checks both fields. Fix: replace `matches\!` with `assert_eq\!(err, BroadcastError::BroadcastFailed { code: 2, message: "transaction already in mempool".to_owned() })` to match the claimed oracle.
